_comment_top: PR-Δ6 (Vercel pilot) — runtime config for the parallel Vercel deploy.
Fly stays primary; Vercel is purely additive. See
_comment_* keys are stripped from runtime; Vercel ignores unknown
top-level keys. JSON has no comment syntax, so we abuse keys.

_comment_runtime: Vercel auto-picks the latest stable Python (3.12 as of
version bumps that could break our pinned cryptography /
pyjwt floors. Bump the pinned runtime via Dependabot's
github-actions ecosystem when a new stable lands.
_comment_memory: 1024 MB is Vercel's default. The app's working set during
load + tool registry) is ~180 MB; 1 GB gives generous
headroom for the multipart-upload path in /api/convert.
Increasing memory also increases vCPU allocation (Vercel's
Fluid Compute model), so smaller `memory` would slow cold
start AND reduce concurrent-request capacity. Defaults stay.
_comment_max_duration: 60s is the Hobby tier's hard ceiling. Most tool calls finish
in <5s (per PR-Δ4's request-ID-correlated logs on Fly).
Long-tail: docx-import → Apps Script Web App round-trip for
large documents can approach 30-45s. The 60s cap is
sufficient for personal-tier traffic; if commercial
activation drives a Pro upgrade (300s max), bump this then.

_comment_regions: Vercel Hobby tier allows ONE region. `iad1` (US East /
Washington DC) is the default and universally available;
selecting Mumbai (`bom1`) would minimize latency for the
operator's region but `bom1` availability on Hobby tier is
not guaranteed (Vercel docs are inconsistent — some routes
exclude it). Defaulting to `iad1` for predictability; the
operator can swap to `bom1` post-deploy if the Hobby tier
accepts it (test by setting `regions: ['bom1']` and watching
Migration to Vercel Pro unlocks multi-region; that's the
performance answer if/when commercial activation drives it.
_comment_env: Env vars are bound via the Vercel dashboard or `vercel env
add` (the operator runbook walks through this). The list
below is documentation of what MUST be set for a working
deploy — Vercel doesn't enforce env-var presence at deploy
time, so a missing var surfaces as a function-runtime
error rather than a deploy-time error.
REQUIRED for any deploy:
- MCP_BEARER_TOKEN — same value Fly uses; the HKDF master.
- GOOGLE_CLIENT_CONFIG — OAuth client secrets JSON.
- GOOGLE_OAUTH_BASE_URL — set to the Vercel deploy URL
(https://<project>-<hash>-<team>.vercel.app or the
operator's custom domain).
- STORAGE_BACKEND — MUST be `vercel_kv` for any per-user
state to persist across cold starts (SqliteBackend on
Vercel's ephemeral tmpfs loses every write).
- KV_REST_API_URL + KV_REST_API_TOKEN — Vercel populates
these automatically when KV is bound to the project. The
operator binds KV via the Vercel dashboard (`Project →
Storage → Create New → KV`) — one click; provisions the
free 30 MB Hobby instance.
OPTIONAL:
- SENTRY_DSN — PR-Δ4 observability; same DSN as Fly.
- LICENSE_KEY_ENFORCEMENT / MCP_LICENSE_KEY — PR-Δ5
commercial-tier; default OFF for personal use.
- GCP_PROJECT_NUMBER — PR-Δ5 GCP project linking for
SOC 2 audit-trail. Optional.
- TRUSTED_HOSTS — comma-separated list. On Vercel,
`*.vercel.app,<custom-domain>` if applicable.
